metadata.summary: Reels-first growth system for Instagram: post 1-2 Reels daily, engage 30 min/day, convert via DM triggers. Hook must work silent. Use 3-5 hashtags, track Reel reach % and engagement rate.

content.title: Instagram Growth Tactics
content.summary: A Reels-first growth system for Instagram: post 1-2 Reels daily, run Stories daily, engage 30 minutes per day, and convert via DM triggers. The hook of a Reel must work with sound off. Use 3-5 hashtags only — 30-hashtag lists risk shadowban. Track Reel reach % and engagement rate, not follower count.

why.p: Instagram's algorithm distributes Reels via Explore to non-followers; feed posts do not get this treatment. Accounts that prioritize Reels with strong visual hooks (text overlay in the first 1-2 seconds) reach audiences 3-10x larger than their follower base.
DM trigger campaigns ("DM me 'GUIDE'") convert 3-5x better than link-in-bio clicks for lead magnets because they start a conversation rather than sending traffic to a cold page.

list.item: Generating Reel script outlines (hook + 3-5 body points + CTA) for a content batch
Drafting weekly caption batches across pillars (educational, BTS, testimonial, CTA)
Writing DM trigger copy for lead generation posts
Producing a weekly content calendar: Reels + Stories + carousels per the schedule
Analyzing an Instagram Insights CSV export for top-performing content patterns
Generating the Reel video itself — agent produces scripts, not video files
Hashtag generation at scale (30 tags) — current safe range is 3-5 targeted tags
Automated commenting on other accounts — Instagram detects bot-style engagement
Audience analysis without an exported Insights CSV — agent cannot pull data directly
Accounts with under 200 followers and no content history — build content foundation first

rule.statement: Prioritize Reels over static feed posts for reach goals. Reels are redistributed via Explore to non-followers; feed posts are not.
rule.rationale: Explore distribution allows accounts to reach audiences 3-10x larger than their follower base. Static posts reach only existing followers.

rule.statement: The hook of a Reel must work with the sound off: text overlay and visual action are mandatory in the first 1-2 seconds.
rule.rationale: A large percentage of mobile users watch video without sound. A sound-dependent hook loses this audience in the first second.

rule.statement: Use 3-5 highly relevant hashtags per post. Never use 30 hashtags — this pattern triggers shadowban risk.
rule.rationale: Instagram's current algorithm gives no additional reach benefit beyond 5 hashtags. Large hashtag lists are a spam signal and can restrict distribution.

list.item: Monday: Educational tip — Reel
Tuesday: Behind the scenes — Story + Reel
Wednesday: Trend or entertainment — Reel
Thursday: Value or tutorial — Reel + Carousel
Friday: Engagement or fun — Reel + Story
Saturday: User content or testimonial — Reel
Sunday: Inspirational or story — Reel

rule.statement: Post Stories daily. Use polls and question boxes daily rather than passive image stories — these are the highest-engagement Story formats.
rule.rationale: Story polls and question boxes drive replies and DMs, which are the highest-value signals for relationship-building and conversion.

rule.statement: Use DM trigger campaigns ("DM me 'GUIDE' to get...") instead of link-in-bio for lead magnets. DM triggers convert 3-5x better than link-in-bio clicks.
rule.rationale: A DM trigger starts a direct conversation with the lead. Link-in-bio sends traffic to a cold page with no relationship context.

example.note: Caption: "I built a 30-day content system that doubled my engagement. DM me 'SYSTEM' and I'll send it to you." This creates a warm DM conversation vs. a cold landing page visit.

antipattern.description: Using ManyChat or non-partner DM automation tools to send bulk automated replies.
antipattern.reason: Non-partner DM automation is against Instagram ToS and risks account suspension. Use only Meta-approved partner tools like ManyChat for keyword triggers.

rule.statement: Track Reel reach % (not just views), engagement rate, Story completion rate, and weekly follower growth rate as primary KPIs.
rule.rationale: Raw view counts are misleading for small accounts. Reach % relative to follower count indicates whether Explore distribution is working.

list.item: Engagement rate: 1% = average, 3% = good, 6%+ = excellent
Reel reach: 10% of followers = average, 30% = good, 100%+ = viral
Story completion: 50% = average, 70% = good, 85%+ = excellent
Follower growth: 1%/week = average, 5%/week = good, 10%+/week = excellent

antipattern.description: Only posting photos instead of Reels.
antipattern.reason: Photos reach only existing followers. Reels reach Explore audiences. Without Reels, organic growth stalls.

antipattern.description: Inconsistent posting cadence — missing days or weeks.
antipattern.reason: Instagram's algorithm penalizes accounts with erratic posting patterns by reducing distribution. Batch-film content to maintain consistency.
Raw, authentic content currently outperforms polished production on Instagram. Audience trust comes from authenticity, not production value.

antipattern.description: No face on camera for personal brand accounts.
antipattern.reason: Accounts showing a real person consistently achieve higher engagement than faceless brand accounts in the same niche.
DM conversations are the highest-conversion touchpoint on Instagram. Delayed responses lose leads that were ready to buy.

rule.statement: Agent-generated Reel scripts must be reviewed for hook quality before filming. The first 1-3 seconds determine watch time; the agent cannot judge if a hook is compelling in delivery.
rule.rationale: Script quality and on-camera delivery are separate skills. Agents produce structure; humans provide authenticity in delivery.

rule.statement: Instagram Graph API provides page-level insights only. Personal account insights require manual export from the Instagram app before agent analysis.
rule.rationale: The API does not expose individual post performance for personal accounts. Agents cannot pull this data autonomously.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Use the agent for two tasks: (1) content batch production — given content pillars, ICP, and the weekly schedule template, produce 14 Reel script hooks + body points + CTAs, 7 Story prompt sequences, and 2-3 carousel outlines per week; (2) performance analysis — given an Instagram Insights CSV export, identify top-performing content types and suggest next week's pillar distribution adjustments. Human films, edits, and posts Reels; agent never touches the upload flow.

p.term: faion-sdd-executor-agent — execute weekly Instagram content production tasks from a spec; password-scrubber-agent — sanitize DM conversation exports before feeding into lead analysis

section.name: prompt-pattern
section.code:
You are an Instagram content strategist for <niche>. ICP: <icp>. Brand voice: <voice>.
Generate this week's Reel scripts: - 2 educational tips (hook + 3 key points + CTA) - 2 behind-the-scenes (narrative arc + authenticity note) - 1 trend/entertainment (format suggestion + hook) - 1 user testimonial (story structure) - 1 inspirational (emotional hook + lesson)
Each script: hook line (max 5 words), body (3-5 bullet points), CTA (specific action), caption (150 chars max), 3 hashtags.

Analyze this Instagram Insights export: <csv_data>
Identify: (1) top 3 Reels by reach %, (2) common hook patterns in high performers, (3) best posting times by engagement, (4) Story completion drop-off points. Recommend content adjustments for next week.

p.term: instagrapi (Python) — Unofficial Instagram API: read insights, schedule posts (pip install instagrapi / https://instagrapi.readthedocs.io); later API — Scheduled posting for Reels, carousels, stories (https://developers.later.com); manychat API — DM automation flows (keyword triggers) (https://developers.manychat.com); ffmpeg — Batch video processing for Reel format compliance (apt install ffmpeg / https://ffmpeg.org); pillow — Auto-generate Story frames and carousel slide images (pip install pillow / https://pillow.readthedocs.io)

Later (SaaS, agent-friendly REST API) — Best scheduling for Instagram Reels; visual planner; ManyChat (SaaS, agent-friendly API) — DM automation for keyword triggers; ToS-compliant approach; Meta Business Suite (SaaS, agent-friendly Graph API) — Official insights export; limited granularity; Canva (SaaS, agent-friendly Apps SDK) — Carousel slide and highlight cover generation; CapCut (SaaS, no API) — Reel editing; manual use; Hootsuite (SaaS, agent-friendly REST API) — Scheduling + analytics; more expensive than Later; Metricool (SaaS, agent-friendly REST API) — Cross-platform analytics including Instagram

list.item: The hook of a Reel must work with the sound off — text overlay and visual action in the first 1-2 seconds are mandatory
Reels get redistributed via Explore; feed posts do not — prioritize Reels over static posts for reach goals
Story polls and question boxes are the highest-engagement Story formats; use them daily rather than passive image stories
DM trigger campaigns ("DM me 'GUIDE'") convert 3-5x better than link-in-bio clicks for lead magnets — use them for every major value offer
Post carousels as the second slide hook (a teaser image that implies more content) — carousel swipe rate is the key metric for feed reach
Batch film content in 2-3 hour sessions weekly; editing and posting daily from scratch leads to inconsistency

p.term: Agent produces scripts and storyboards; the filming and editing step is entirely human — plan for this production time when generating volume
ManyChat-style automation is permitted under Instagram's official partner program, but non-partner tools (instagrapi DM sending) are against ToS and risk account suspension
Agent should never generate 30-hashtag lists; 3-5 highly relevant hashtags is the current safe range
Instagram Graph API provides page-level insights only; personal account insights require manual export from the app
Reel distribution rules (audio usage, original audio vs trending audio) change frequently; agent recommendations reflect training data, not current algorithm state

template.purpose: Agent prompt: weekly Reel scripts (7 types, hook + body + CTA)
Generate 10-slide carousel text outline from topic + key points
